Current language

Projects where research takes form.

PROJECTS

Projects where research takes form.

Electronic Artefacts develops client products, proprietary platforms, open-source tools and cultural works. Each project is documented with its role, maturity and relationships so visitors can distinguish a live delivery from an active prototype, a public software artefact, a long-term platform or an artistic publication.

Client products under real constraints.

L’Œil de Meg connects a public photography portfolio with client operations. AtypikHouse explores discovery, booking, host tools and traveller accounts. Both show how brand, content, UX and software become one service.

Platforms built around connected knowledge.

Vestiges is the flagship public application of VASTE. It maps living know-how across people, practices, materials, places and institutions, treating identity, provenance and explicit relationships as product features.

Open-source browser tools.

Voice Capture Studio standardizes local speech recording, voice-over and dubbing workflows through a live browser app, public repository and structured WAV plus metadata exports.

Cultural work with an accumulating archive.

Palimpsests is a five-act album cycle by ORETH about memory, inheritance and transmission. Music, writing, imagery and archive records remain connected so the work can grow without losing its context.

Every project keeps its context.

Project pages expose status, dates, media, related programs and research fields. The portfolio becomes evidence of delivery and a navigable record of how ideas move between commissions, proprietary technology, open software and publishing.

Explore programsRead the researchStart a conversation
